sources_audited.note: The Census product list assigns washing and cleaning products to 811192 and explicitly covers automatic equipment, manual labor, detailing, hand washing, and self-service formats. It supports the cited format and classification facts; the numerical a, e, and o values remain disclosed proxy or estimate judgments.

O*NET lists the cited physical cleaning, inspection, inventory, machine-monitoring, activation, handling, and minor-repair tasks for Cleaners of Vehicles and Equipment. The packet correctly discloses that this occupation is not a wage-weighted 811192 staffing mix.

The ICA page reports nearly eight in ten customers willing to accept a value- and quality-dependent price increase, nearly 90% member renewal intent, continued membership growth, and rising saturation and competitive density. These facts support the directional rationale, not a measured retained-savings share.

The ICA-hosted Rinsed report states 5.2% same-location combined revenue growth, 13.2% member-revenue growth, a 7.1% retail-revenue decline, and $30 average monthly member revenue. It supports the membership-mix claims but does not directly measure AI implementation or savings retention.

BizBuySell reports 1,286 sold listings, 207 median days on market, and says the data cover auto repair and service businesses sold on and reported to its marketplace. The packet accurately identifies the broader population and its mismatch to LMM car washes.

FHWA reports baseline light-duty VMT growth of 0.6% annually for 2023-2043 and 0.5% for 2023-2053. The d5 bridge transparently converts this national travel forecast into a near-flat five-year car-wash-demand proxy and states the likely overstatement direction.

The February 25, 2025 ICA page reports a definitive agreement for Whistle Express to acquire Take 5 Car Wash and a combined 530 locations across 23 states upon closing. It supports transaction-channel existence, not a denominator-based LMM transfer rate, as disclosed.

caveats: The s5 range is only weakly anchored: S5 is a broader, smaller-business marketplace sample and S7 is one large platform deal, so neither supplies a car-wash denominator or five-year transfer incidence. The bridge discloses this and the value is best read as bounded judgment; changing its evidence label alone would not change the MODEL_CONDITIONED action, and a plausible correction within the registered range does not change the base LOW_PRIORITY tier.

The q sources measure renewal intent, price tolerance, and membership-versus-retail revenue trends rather than the fraction of AI-enabled savings retained by an operator. The ESTIMATE label is honest, but C's capped base score should not be read as directly observed pricing power.

The a proxy relies on one broad vehicle-and-equipment-cleaner occupation rather than the wage-weighted employment mix of 811192, while the included formats range from hand detailing to unattended self-service. The packet discloses both limitations and uses a wide H range, but site-format diligence is essential.

The lens marks the industry as non-heterogeneous even though the narrative correctly emphasizes materially different labor and operating models across detailing, hand-wash, tunnel, mobile, and self-service formats. This metadata inconsistency does not alter the inclusive default lens, the score, or the action.
summary: Mechanics pass and all seven score-driving sources are reachable and support the factual claims for which they are cited. Evidence states, bridges, construct placement, ranges, and conclusions are broadly honest. The record is useful as a LOW_PRIORITY, MODEL_CONDITIONED screen, with important disclosed uncertainty in wage-weighted AI exposure, transfer incidence, savings retention, and format mix; none of the identified weaknesses passes the v5 materiality test for rejection.
